old_claim.note: Laboratory Diagnostic Medicine provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: HELP's application programs were organized around database, decision-making, manual-entry, and communications subsystems, making systematic software development an enabling foundation.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract description of HELP application-program subsystems and clinical applications including laboratory, x-ray, pharmacy, and records.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The HELP article describes application programs built from database-management, decision-making, manual-entry, and communications subsystems, with laboratory as one application area.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports software engineering as the enabling platform discipline because the system is implemented as coordinated application programs and rejects laboratory diagnostics as merely one served clinical workflow.

ontology_before: Hospital information systems were modeled through laboratory diagnostics, confusing a hospital department workflow with the software platform.
ontology_after: Hospital information systems are modeled through software and database infrastructure that can serve laboratory, imaging, pharmacy, records, and administrative workflows.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: hospital_information_systems no longer directly depends on laboratory_diagnostic_medicine and now depends on software_engineering.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to laboratory information systems rather than broad HIS.
A source showed the broad hospital information-system platform requires laboratory diagnostic medicine as a method prerequisite.
The graph reintroduced laboratory_diagnostic_medicine as a direct HIS prerequisite.
short_reason: Laboratory diagnostics is a workflow HIS can serve; software is the implementation foundation.
